title: Neutral-Atom Quantum Computing Market 2026-2036: QuEra-Google, Atom Computing-Microsoft, and Pasqal Drive 1,000-to-Million-Qubit Roadmap Toward Fault-Tolerant Systems
why:
- Neutral-atom quantum computing enables scalable 2D and 3D qubit arrays with minimal crosstalk, leveraging well-understood atomic physics and room-temperature operation.
- Companies such as QuEra (partnered with Google), Atom Computing (partnered with Microsoft), and Pasqal (which reached 1,000 qubits in 2024) are rapidly advancing the technology toward thousands and eventually millions of qubits.
- The technology roadmap projects fault-tolerant quantum computing with 100,000+ qubits by 2029-2030, and million-qubit, fully fault-tolerant systems for industrial deployment by 2032-2035.
- Neutral-atom systems currently demonstrate long coherence times and high single- and two-qubit fidelity, with key application areas in quantum simulation, chemistry, optimization, and machine learning, appealing especially to pharmaceutical, chemical, and financial industries.
- The field is attracting significant investment from major tech companies and governments, establishing neutral-atom platforms as serious competitors to superconducting qubit systems.
